Off Calendar Jun-21-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-13-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Hill for and with Gail Fisher (Administrator).

Mr. Hill stated that funds not being placed in an interest bearing account was an oversight, that the request for the $8,500.00 reserve for payment of accounting fees was withdrawn in the declaration filed on March 27th, and that they are only asking for a $20,000.00 income tax reserve, $3,000.00 for tax preparation, $1,595.00 for the heir search company and $435.00 for filing fees. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is taken under submission. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-31-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-17-2017 Continued To May-31-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear on May 31, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why they failed to respond to the Examiner's notes dated April 3, 2017. The First and Final Account, etc. is also continued to that date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Tim Kneis. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-24-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Hill for and with (petitioners) Gail Fisher & Rosalind Fisher.

The court stated that it has received the additional evidence, that one of the declarations state that this is what the decedent wanted which will allow the court to admit the will to probate, that there are still no objections, that the court is inclined to think that there is a written witnessed document that appears to be testamentary intent and addressed the issue of bond indicating that the value of the property ($445,000.00)seemed low. Mr. Hill stated that the value was estimated by a real estate agent, that the property is run down and has no kitchen, that there is a squatter with vicious dogs and stated that once they have an administrator in place they will deal with the squatter. The Court granted the petition for letters of administration with will annexed and signed the order in open court. Bond is set at $445,000.00 and counsel is instructed to petition for increase of bond as the value of property increases. Status of filing a petition for final distribution is set for June 21, 2017, 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Continued To Jun-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar May-13-2015 Continued To Jun-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Hill for and with Rosalind Fisher. Gail Fisher also present.

Mr. Hill reported that they have been unable to find the subscribing witnesses and that they believe that it is Ms. Horne's signature. The Court stated that there are provisions in the Probate Code for allowance and that there should be a way to prove the will. Mr. Hill stated that he will do more research. The Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed was continued to June 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputies Lopes & Santiago. (Not Reported)
